programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa and Stephen navigate their new pregnancy. Dave tries to connect with his son. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Yorkshire to help the Mann-Monro family, a blended household of six that has been overwhelmed by years of clutter and emotional memories.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Irita Marriott chase two high-energy teams around a packed antiques fair in the Scottish Borders town of Kelso.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden lives of foxes, hedgehogs, blue tits and frogs, each making a life for themselves in a small urban garden in Bristol.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Arsenal travel to Manchester City in a game that could be crucial in deciding this season's Premier League title race. Elsewhere, Everton face Liverpool at their new Hill Dickinson Stadium.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan takes a trip through the BBC archives to look at the lives and careers of Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins, Jimmy 'Whirlwind' White and Ronnie 'the Rocket' O'Sullivan.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael is in Kagoshima, a city which sits in the shadow of Japan's most active volcano.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.

programme.title: Mastermind Final
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions in the grand final. Subjects are Dame Julie Andrews, The Films of Danny Boyle, Beatrix Potter, Robert Burns, Notre-Dame Cathedral and Jim Lovell.

programme.title: University Challenge Final
programme.desc: It's the grand final. Who will lift the series trophy? Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Great British Menu: The Finals
programme.desc: The eight finalists compete to win the starter course at a banquet celebrating the British film industry. Director Marli Siu is the guest judge.

programme.title: Charmain and the Prophet
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar travels to Ghana to look into Charmain's death. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: At the peak of fame, Michael is accused of abuse, and his business empire risks collapse. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights of the snooker action on day three of the World Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest in the Midlands as it reaches a milestone moment: the planting of its 10-millionth tree.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Forensics must identify the attacker after a stabbed teen is left fighting for his life. Contains some strong language and upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing toward the remote riverside town of Halfeti, where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom revisits textile weaver Shiv and portrait artist Jill to find out how they built life-changing businesses after leaving his business boot camp and how much they make from them.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of families go antiques-hunting at a huge fair in Kent, in the company of their experts Catherine Southon and JP, aka Jonathan Pratt.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: A criminal gang targets northern farms, a huge barn fire threatens cattle in Leicestershire, and Paul Colgan reunites a farmer with the air ambulance crew who saved her life.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists search for a beloved ring that was lost on a football field in 1966.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 4 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan looks at the legends of the green baize who helped make snooker both a national obsession and a television phenomenon.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 4 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes the ferry to the Amakusa archipelago, visits the town where missionaries brought Christianity to Japan and meets a master swordsmith in Shin Omuta.

programme.title: Great British Menu: The Finals
programme.desc: The eight finalists compete to win the fish course at a banquet celebrating notable Britons of the past and Great British Menu's 20th year. Actor Will Poulter is the guest judge.

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship: Goes to Sea
programme.desc: With just a few weeks to go before HMS Queen Elizabeth arrives in New York in spectacular style, the F35 jets try a method of landing never attempted before.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies are joined by Neil Delamere, Cariad Lloyd and Chris McCausland for a vulgar episode of QI. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann delves deeper into the minds of serial killers while balancing life as a mother. Contains sexual violence and disturbing scenes.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob venture to southern Ireland to fish the mighty Lough Corrib.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann's landmark study reshapes criminal investigation. Serial crime falls, but the battle is only just beginning. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits with Bill Bailey
programme.desc: Jordan and Cian are champion fundraisers aiming to beat dementia.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights from the fourth day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Linda Walsh, Conor's one true love, is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: A report on global heating could rule out some key host cities out of the tournament. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It's week 12, and the finalists must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry experts before Lord Sugar finally decides who will become his next business partner.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: The Apprentice has a winner! Angela hears from Lord Sugar and his new business partner.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
